maps.name: Acute Myocardial Infarction (STEMI)
maps.definition: Acute coronary occlusion causing ischemic myocardial necrosis.
maps.cascade: Atherosclerosis → Plaque rupture → Thrombus → Coronary occlusion → Ischemia → Necrosis → ↓Contractility → ↓CO → Cardiogenic shock
maps.presentation: Chest pain, diaphoresis, dyspnea, ST elevation.

maps.name: Heart Failure (Acute Decompensated)
maps.definition: Acute inability to maintain perfusion without congestion.
maps.cascade: ↓CO → RAAS/SNS activation → Na/H2O retention → ↑Preload → Pulmonary/peripheral congestion → End-organ hypoperfusion
maps.tips: Track response by urine output + symptoms.

maps.name: Cardiogenic Shock
maps.definition: Critical hypoperfusion due to pump failure.
maps.etiology: Large MI, severe valve failure, myocarditis.
maps.cascade: Massive pump failure → ↓↓CO → ↓MAP → Compensatory vasoconstriction → ↑SVR/afterload → further ↓CO → organ failure

maps.name: Cardiac Tamponade
maps.definition: Pericardial pressure impairs diastolic filling.
maps.cascade: Pericardial fluid ↑ → chamber compression → ↓filling → ↓SV/CO → obstructive shock

maps.name: ARDS
maps.definition: Inflammatory non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema.
maps.cascade: Insult → Cytokines → Membrane damage → Permeability edema → Surfactant loss → Collapse → V/Q mismatch → refractory hypoxemia

maps.name: Pulmonary Embolism
maps.definition: Pulmonary arterial obstruction by thrombus.
maps.cascade: DVT → embolus → ↑PVR → RV strain/dilation → ↓LV preload → ↓CO → obstructive shock

maps.name: Status Asthmaticus
maps.definition: Severe refractory bronchospasm.
maps.cascade: Trigger → bronchospasm + mucus + inflammation → air trapping → intrinsic PEEP → hypercapnia → acidosis → failure

maps.name: Tension Pneumothorax
maps.definition: One-way pleural air accumulation causing shock.
maps.cascade: One-way leak → pressure rises → lung collapse → mediastinal shift → venous return falls → shock/PEA
maps.tips: Treat first, image later if unstable.

maps.name: Sepsis Cascade
maps.definition: Dysregulated host response to infection causing organ dysfunction.
maps.cascade: Infection → cytokine storm → vasodilation/permeability → distributive shock → hypoperfusion → lactate rise → MODS

maps.name: Anaphylaxis
maps.definition: Severe systemic hypersensitivity reaction.
maps.cascade: Allergen → histamine release → vasodilation + bronchospasm + leak → distributive shock + airway risk

maps.name: Hypovolemic Shock
maps.definition: Shock from volume loss.
maps.cascade: Loss of volume → ↓preload/SV/CO → compensatory vasoconstriction → hypoperfusion → lactic acidosis
maps.medical: Crystalloid, blood products, TXA when indicated.
maps.tips: Class III-IV needs aggressive blood strategy.

maps.name: Ischemic Stroke
maps.definition: Acute cerebral infarction from arterial occlusion.
maps.cascade: Occlusion → penumbra/core infarct → edema → ↑ICP/herniation risk
maps.medical: tPA up to 4.5h, thrombectomy up to 24h selected.

maps.name: Raised Intracranial Pressure
maps.definition: Pathologic elevation of ICP causing reduced CPP.
maps.cascade: Volume increase → ↑ICP → ↓CPP → ischemia → edema cycle → herniation
maps.presentation: Headache, vomiting, decreased GCS, Cushing triad.

maps.name: Status Epilepticus
maps.definition: Seizure >5 min or recurrent without recovery.
maps.etiology: Medication nonadherence, CNS insults, metabolic causes.
maps.cascade: Prolonged seizure → excitotoxicity → neuronal injury → edema/systemic complications
maps.medical: Benzodiazepine then second-line ASM then infusion anesthesia.

maps.name: Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA)
maps.definition: Hyperglycemic ketosis with metabolic acidosis due to insulin deficiency.
maps.cascade: Insulin deficit → ketogenesis + hyperglycemia → osmotic diuresis → dehydration/electrolyte loss → acidosis
maps.tips: Replace K before insulin if K<3.3.

maps.name: Hepatic Encephalopathy
maps.definition: Neuropsychiatric dysfunction from liver failure toxins.
maps.cascade: Liver failure → ammonia rise → BBB crossing → astrocyte swelling → AMS/coma

maps.name: Rhabdomyolysis
maps.definition: Skeletal muscle breakdown releasing nephrotoxic contents.
maps.cascade: Muscle lysis → CK/myoglobin/K release → tubular injury → AKI + hyperkalemia
maps.nursing: Aggressive fluids and frequent K checks.
maps.medical: IV hydration, treat electrolytes, dialysis if needed.

maps.name: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI)
maps.definition: Abrupt decline in kidney function and filtration.
maps.cascade: Cause insult → ↓GFR → azotemia/fluid-electrolyte derangements → uremic effects
maps.medical: Cause-directed therapy and RRT when indicated.

maps.name: DIC
maps.definition: Systemic coagulation activation causing clotting and bleeding.
maps.cascade: Trigger → thrombin surge → microthrombi + factor consumption → bleeding + ischemia

maps.name: Multi-Organ Dysfunction Syndrome (MODS)
maps.definition: Progressive failure of ≥2 organ systems after severe insult.
maps.cascade: Primary insult → SIRS → sequential organ failures → escalating inflammation → death risk
maps.complications: High mortality with more failed organs.
maps.tips: Early reversal of first failing organ matters.
